title: Precursor information lost when building RerrAction
description:
File: python/src/lichen/loadng/error.py
Line: 83

When building a RerrAction, the precursor set is popped (deleted) from the dictionary:
```python
def _build_action(self, dest: IPv6Address, error_code: int = 0) -> RerrAction:
    precursors = self._precursors.pop(dest, set())  # Destructive!
    ...
```

Problem: If the caller fails to successfully transmit the RERR to all precursors (network failure, radio busy, etc.), the precursor information is permanently lost. There is no way to retry notification.

Impact:
1. Failed RERR transmission leaves upstream nodes with stale routes
2. No recovery mechanism - precursor info must be rebuilt via record_flow()
3. Silent data loss that can cause routing black holes

Fix: Either:
1. Make _build_action non-destructive and let caller explicitly clear precursors after successful transmission
2. Return precursors from RerrAction.notify without removing them, add explicit clear_precursors(dest) method
3. Add a commit/rollback pattern

Example non-destructive approach:
```python
def _build_action(self, dest: IPv6Address, error_code: int = 0) -> RerrAction:
    precursors = self._precursors.get(dest, set())  # Don't pop
    ...

def clear_precursors(self, dest: IPv6Address) -> None:
    self._precursors.pop(dest, None)
```
close_reason: Made _build_action non-destructive with .get().copy(); added clear_precursors(); updated docs. Tests and ruff pass. Commit 2416e7a1c
